basics.description: Deploy Keysight Vision Orchestrator (KVO) v3.0.0-55 from Azure Marketplace. KVO is the centralized control and automation layer for Keysight Visibility solutions, managing virtual Packet Broker (vPB) fleets across physical, virtual, and cloud environments. After deployment, the web UI is reachable in about 60 seconds and full initialization completes in roughly 15 minutes. Refer to Keysight KVO documentation for default UI credentials.

location.label: Region
location.toolTip: Azure region for deployment. Pick the region closest to the vPB fleet you plan to orchestrate.

elements.name: vmName
elements.label: VM name
elements.toolTip: Name of the KVO VM. Used as the computer name and as a prefix for the public IP, NSG, NIC, and (when creating one) the VNet.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 3 to 64 characters. Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only. Must start and end with a letter or digit.

elements.name: adminUsername
elements.label: Linux admin username
elements.toolTip: OS-level admin user for SSH. The KVO web UI uses its own separate admin account (refer to Keysight KVO documentation for default UI credentials).

toolTip.password: 12+ characters with uppercase, lowercase, a digit, and a symbol. This is the OS-level password, not the KVO web UI password.

elements.name: vmSize
elements.label: VM size
elements.toolTip: Standard_D4s_v5 (4 vCPU, 16 GB) is recommended for most deployments. Scale up for environments orchestrating many vPB fleets.

allowedValues.label: Standard_D16s_v5 (16 vCPU, 64 GB, very large fleets)

elements.name: vnetMode
elements.label: Virtual network
elements.toolTip: Create a new VNet sized below, or attach to a VNet you already own.

elements.name: addressSpace
elements.label: VNet address space (CIDR)
elements.toolTip: Address space for the new VNet. Must not overlap with VNets you plan to peer with.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid CIDR block (for example, 10.60.0.0/16).

elements.name: subnetPrefix
elements.label: Subnet prefix (CIDR)
elements.toolTip: Subnet prefix for the KVO NIC. Must be contained within the VNet address space.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid CIDR block (for example, 10.60.1.0/24).

elements.name: existingVnetName
elements.label: Existing VNet name
elements.toolTip: Name of the existing VNet to deploy into.

elements.name: existingVnetResourceGroup
elements.label: Existing VNet resource group
elements.toolTip: Resource group of the existing VNet. Leave blank to use this deployment's resource group.

elements.name: existingSubnetName
elements.label: Existing subnet name
elements.toolTip: Subnet within the existing VNet to attach the KVO NIC to.
